title: Fix incidents failing to load (frontend-to-backend connection)

requirements.summary: Diagnose and fix the frontend-to-backend connection so that incidents load successfully on both the Dashboard and Admin Panel, eliminating 'Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend' errors by auditing the full data path including canister ID configuration, useActor hook initialization, useQueries hook invocation, and provider ordering
acceptanceCriteria: The Dashboard loads and displays incidents from the backend without showing any error message.
The Admin Panel loads incident data from the backend without showing any error message.
The useActor hook returns a valid actor instance for anonymous (public) queries.
The getAllIncidents React Query hook successfully calls the backend and returns incident data.
No console errors related to canister ID, actor initialization, or backend connection appear on page load.
The light/dark mode ThemeToggle continues to function correctly alongside the working backend connection.
Internet Identity authentication on the /admin route continues to function correctly.

file_operations.description: Fix the getAllIncidents query by ensuring it correctly handles the actor instance: add null check for actor before calling getAllIncidents, ensure enabled flag properly waits for actor availability, add error handling for backend connection failures, and verify the query returns data in the correct format expected by Dashboard and AdminPanel components

Add robust error handling and loading state management for the incidents query: ensure the component properly waits for both actor initialization and data fetching, add user-friendly error messages distinguishing between actor initialization failures and data fetch failures, verify the incidents array is safely handled when undefined or null, and add console logging for debugging the data flow from useGetAllIncidents to rendering

Add comprehensive error handling and loading state management for the incidents query in the admin context: ensure the component waits for both authentication and actor initialization before fetching data, add clear error messaging for backend connection issues distinct from authentication failures, verify that all form components receive valid incident data arrays, and add debug logging to trace the authenticated actor creation and query execution flow

Add defensive null checks and error handling for the actor-dependent ID validation: ensure the isIdAlreadyUsed query properly handles null or undefined actor instances, add appropriate loading and error states for the ID uniqueness check, prevent form submission when actor is unavailable, and provide clear user feedback when backend validation cannot be performed due to connection issues

Add error handling for actor availability in the incident selection and update submission flow: ensure the incidents dropdown safely handles loading and error states from useGetAllIncidents, add null checks before accessing actor-dependent data, provide user feedback when backend connection is unavailable, and verify the mutation properly waits for a valid actor instance

Add robust error handling for actor-dependent incident loading and status changes: ensure the incident selector safely handles null or undefined incidents array, add appropriate loading states while fetching incident data, provide clear error messages when backend is unavailable, and verify the status change mutation waits for actor initialization before attempting the operation
